# 1.0.0-alpha.11

- Implemented BL-BENCH-046.
- Connected all 72 nodes and all 16 gates into one canonical route.
- Added fail-closed connectivity validator, report, official utility SVG and 5 acceptance tests.
- Epic gates continue to subsequent epics; only explicit blocked/final terminal routes terminate execution.

# 1.0.0-alpha.9

- Implemented `BL-BENCH-045`.
- Added deterministic package-level self-validation and fail-closed release disposition.
- Added selected-run cross-artifact consistency, foreign-literal, validator-receipt, version/reference, approval-binding, Manual QA, Automation and Driver terminal checks.
- Added six acceptance fixtures; all pass.
- Backlog is 45/45 DONE.

# 1.0.0-alpha.8

- Added OPEN task `BL-BENCH-045 — Mandatory Result Package Self-Validation and Release Gate`.
- Preserved the RUN_02 detailed analysis as the source incident attachment.
- Added a Jira-ready ticket.
- Backlog is now 44 DONE / 1 OPEN.

# 1.0.0-alpha.7

- Completed BL-BENCH-044.
- Added enforceable benchmark evidence-base governance runtime.
- Added run acceptance schema, policy, templates and canonical scoreboard.
- Added 5/5 positive/negative acceptance tests.
- Backlog is 44/44 DONE.

## 1.0.0-alpha.3 — 2026-07-16

- Added `BL-BENCH-042` as OPEN.
- Registered mandatory use of the current Ordo language package and applicable ARF meta-playbook during playbook creation, modification, validation and release.
- Explicitly removed any claim that the current alpha already executes ARF as a mandatory runtime.
- Backlog is now 41 DONE / 1 OPEN.

# Playbook Changelog

## v0.10.0 — Epic 10 Diagnostics

- Completed `BL-BENCH-032..034`.
- Added causal diagnostic request and case contracts.
- Added evidence hierarchy, corroboration statuses and counterfactual checks.
- Added versioned root-cause taxonomy and machine-readable registry.
- Preserved original execution evidence as immutable during diagnosis.
- Full improvement and regression workflow remains open.

## 0.1.0-working-draft — 2026-07-16

### Added

- Complete consolidated backlog `BL-BENCH-001..040` with status ledger.
- Benchmark Playbook Contract.
- Benchmark Domain Vocabulary.
- Benchmark Conceptual Model.
- Initial design decisions.
- Initial Ordo authoring Process Rail skeleton.
- Self-validation report and package manifests.

### Completed

- Epic 01 / `BL-BENCH-001`.
- Epic 01 / `BL-BENCH-002`.

### Known gaps

- Task classes and test-case intake not yet materialized.
- RUN contracts not yet materialized.
- Package variants, Drivers, evaluators and result registry are not implemented.
- Package is not execution-ready.


## v0.2.0 — Epic 02: Task Classes and Test Cases

- Completed `BL-BENCH-003..005`.
- Added canonical TC01/TC02/TC03 class model and selection gate.
- Added deterministic test-case creation intake.
- Added test-case source package contract, YAML templates and JSON schema.
- Classified the current database-change benchmark as TC03 primary / TC02 secondary.
- Added `BL-BENCH-041` for future scoped YAML patch verification.
- Extended the Ordo Process Rail by adding Epic 02 nodes without replacing Epic 01 nodes.
\n\n## v0.3.0 — Epic 03 RUN Scenario Model\n\n- Completed `BL-BENCH-006` and `BL-BENCH-007`.\n- Added canonical contracts for RUN_01–RUN_05.\n- Added terminal vocabulary, fact-status lifecycle, correction and artifact rules.\n- Added RUN registry, reusable RUN template, extension proposal template and JSON Schema.\n- Added admission/versioning/migration rules for RUN_06+.\n- Extended cumulative Ordo Process Rail without deleting Epic 01–02 nodes.\n- Kept `BL-BENCH-041` open; its scoped YAML patch verification is not claimed as implemented.\n

## v0.4.0 — Epic 04 Package Variants

- Completed BL-BENCH-008..012.
- Added four-variant registry and manifest schema/template.
- Added compiler contracts for YAML, structured instructions and historical all-in-one.
- Added strict direct-domain adaptation boundary.
- Preserved BL-BENCH-041 as open; no executable scoped-patch verifier is claimed.


## 0.5.0-working-draft — 2026-07-16

### Added

- Step-Bound Driver contract.
- Adaptive Semantic Driver contract.
- Deterministic Driver Selection Gate and binding schema/template.
- Blind Isolation rules and isolation manifest template.
- Driver registry.
- Epic 05 Ordo nodes, gate and terminal.

### Status

- Epic 05 completed.
- Production Driver runtime and real benchmark launch remain open.
- `BL-BENCH-041` remains open.


## v0.8.0 — Epic 07 Benchmark Execution

- Added universal launch manifest and immutable attempt identity.
- Added deterministic integrity/isolation/residue preflight gate.
- Added append-only hash-chain execution logging contract.
- Added terminal route handling for completed, input-blocked, exhausted, not-ready and no-go.
- Marked `BL-BENCH-017..020` DONE; `BL-BENCH-041` remains OPEN.
- Production launcher/runner is not claimed.


## v0.8.0 — Epic 07 Process Validation

- Added independent weighted Process Quality Contract.
- Added canonical Process Failure Caps registry.
- Added process evaluation template and JSON Schema.
- Added Epic 07 nodes/gate/terminal to the cumulative Ordo rail.
- Kept BL-BENCH-041 open.


## v0.8.0 — Epic 08 Document Validation

- Added versioned document-quality contracts for Passport, Jira, Implementation Prompt, Manual QA and Automation.
- Added corrected Jira criteria: no mandatory unit-test detail, references to Passport allowed, no duplication requirement, section order not scored.
- Added artifact-specific failure caps and focused artifact review gate.
- Added machine-readable evaluation report template/schema.


## v0.9.0 — Epic 09 Results and Comparison

- Completed `BL-BENCH-028..031`.
- Added append-only result registry identity and lifecycle policy.
- Added reproducible comparative matrix contract and build metadata.
- Added result supersession, re-evaluation, invalidation and recalculation rules.
- Added cross-variant comparability, fairness, stability and winner-declaration gates.
- Kept `BL-BENCH-041` open.
- No real benchmark records or production registry/matrix executable are claimed.


## v0.11.0 — Epic 11

- Added improvement patch workflow.
- Added deterministic regression scenario selection.
- Added hard five-cycle improvement campaign mode.
- Added stable benchmark evolution and cohort compatibility rules.
- `BL-BENCH-035..038` marked DONE; `BL-BENCH-041` remains OPEN.


## 0.12.0 — Epic 12

- Completed BL-BENCH-039 and BL-BENCH-040.
- Added evidence-package policy, manifests, schemas and index.
- Added deterministic handoff package and receiver preflight.
- BL-BENCH-041 remains open.


## v0.13.0 — BL-BENCH-041 closure
Scoped YAML patch verification is enforceable through node allowlists, canonical structural hashes, semantic diffs, out-of-scope rejection and persisted proof reports. Backlog complete: 41/41.


## 1.0.0-alpha.3
- Implemented BL-BENCH-042 mandatory Ordo language-package and ARF meta-runtime integration.
- Added fail-closed binding, execution state, validation evidence and release gate.


## 1.0.0-alpha.4

- Added `BL-BENCH-043 — External/Internal Validation Contract Alignment Gate` as OPEN.
- Requires fail-closed rule-level equivalence between external canonical validators and node-local/template validation rules.
- Requires regeneration and revalidation of a document-generation node before it can be accepted.

## 1.0.0-alpha.5
- Closed BL-BENCH-043 with enforceable external/internal validation alignment gate, regeneration loop and release evidence.


## 1.0.0-alpha.7 — 2026-07-16

- Added OPEN task `BL-BENCH-044 — Benchmark Evidence and Run Acceptance Governance`.
- Added Jira-ready ticket under `jira/`.
- Preserved the complete owner-provided process as a checksum-bound backlog attachment and normative task description.
- No implementation or DONE claim is made for BL-BENCH-044.


## 1.0.0-alpha.10 — 2026-07-16

- Added OPEN task `BL-BENCH-046 — Canonical Execution Graph Connectivity and Reachability Gate`.
- Requires one canonical connected execution route, explicit epic-boundary transitions, classification of all 72 nodes, and fail-closed rejection of orphan, unreachable, dangling and accidental dead-end nodes.
- Requires machine-readable connectivity evidence and SVG generated from the same validated source revision.
- No implementation or DONE claim is made for BL-BENCH-046.


## 1.0.0-alpha.12 — 2026-07-16

- Added OPEN task `BL-BENCH-047 — Mandatory Black-Box End-to-End Pre-Release Self-Validation and Run Evidence`.
- Requires sealed-candidate execution of RUN_01–RUN_05 by a blind external harness.
- Requires Driver-mediated validator receipts, correction loops, expected terminal-state verification and final-ZIP consumer validation.
- Requires five immutable checksum-bound pre-release run-evidence archives.
- Preserved the RUN_02 `NO_CHANGE / INCOMPLETE_VALIDATION_HARD_STOP` incident as source evidence and a future regression fixture.
- No implementation or successful black-box campaign is claimed.

- alpha.13: BL-BENCH-047 implemented with sealed-candidate deterministic campaign and five evidence ZIPs.


## 1.0.0-alpha.14

- Registered `BL-BENCH-048` as OPEN.
- Defined the internal dry evaluation, scoring, diagnostic and user acceptance phase before external blind testing.
- Required strict separation between internal development evidence and canonical blind benchmark evidence.
- Added a Jira-ready issue specification.


## Alpha 15 — BL-BENCH-048
Implemented internal dry evaluation and explicit user acceptance gate before external blind testing.


## 1.0.0-alpha.16 — 2026-07-17

- Added OPEN task `BL-BENCH-049 — Canonical Evaluation Methodology and Test-Case-Specific Evaluation Profiles`.
- Requires a general methodology at evidence-package root and a mandatory inherited profile for every test case.
- Requires deterministic effective-methodology compilation and SHA-256 binding in every audit and score ledger record.
- Requires fail-closed rejection of missing, stale, incompatible or weakening overrides.
- No implementation or migration of existing test cases is claimed.

## 1.0.0-alpha.17
- Closed BL-BENCH-049.
- Added canonical evaluation methodology, profile inheritance/compiler, binding gate and acceptance tests.


## 1.0.0-alpha.20 — 2026-07-17

- Registered `BL-BENCH-050` as OPEN.
- Added a general fail-closed requirement that the exact sealed archive must execute all verification criteria it declares for external consumers.
- Added missing-validator, incompatible-entry-point, undeclared-equivalent and final-ZIP retest acceptance criteria.

Implemented declared-contract archive self-application as decision-tree nodes N074–N078 and hard gate G_DECLARED_CONTRACT_ARCHIVE_RELEASE.

## 1.0.0-alpha.22 — 2026-07-17

- 1.0.0-alpha.22: completed BL-BENCH-051 representation compilation governance.


## 1.0.0-alpha.24 — 2026-07-17

- Added OPEN task `BL-BENCH-053 — Improvement Plateau and Best Confirmed Version Retention Gate`.
- Requires measurable candidate-versus-confirmed improvement before replacement.
- Requires retention of the best confirmed artifact and terminal `IMPROVEMENT_PLATEAU_REACHED` when no gain is proven.
- Prohibits identical correction retries without new evidence, facts or strategy.
- Preserved the normative source rule as a checksum-bound backlog attachment.


## alpha.24
- BL-BENCH-052 implemented: evidence catalog construction, lifecycle, score eligibility, manifest parity and restorable transfer governance.

## 1.0.0-alpha.25

- Registered `BL-BENCH-054 — Execution Progress Status Output Governance` as OPEN.
- Added normative one-line progress format, canonical status set, evidence-binding and no-reasoning-disclosure requirements.
- Added Jira-ready ticket and preserved source rule.
- No runtime implementation is claimed in this release.


## Alpha 26 — BL-BENCH-053
Implemented improvement plateau governance, measurable delta comparison, protected regression prevention, best confirmed version retention, and terminal loop exit.
